title: Employer Student Loan Repayment Assistance Programs

requirements_for_qualification.summary: Must establish and maintain qualified educational assistance program allowing employer to provide tax-free student loan repayment benefits up to annual limits as employee compensation alternative
criteria: Employer must establish written educational assistance program under IRC §127 requirements
Program must not discriminate in favor of highly compensated employees under nondiscrimination testing
Student loan repayment assistance treated as educational assistance under expanded §127 definition
Annual limit: $5,250 per employee for combined educational assistance and student loan repayments
Benefits paid directly to loan servicer or reimbursed to employee for qualified student loan payments
Qualified student loans include loans incurred for employee's own qualified higher education expenses
Program must provide reasonable notification to eligible employees about availability and terms
Provision currently effective through 2025; sunset provisions may require congressional extension for future years

student_loan_assistance_rules.annual_limit: $5,250 maximum per employee per year tax-free; combined with tuition assistance if applicable
student_loan_assistance_rules.tax_free_to_employee: Qualified payments excluded from employee wages and not subject to income or payroll taxes
student_loan_assistance_rules.employer_deduction: Employer deducts payments as ordinary and necessary business expense (compensation equivalent)
student_loan_assistance_rules.direct_payment_method: Payment to loan servicer or reimbursement to employee for documented student loan payments
student_loan_assistance_rules.nondiscrimination_testing: Plan must not favor highly compensated employees; testing required annually
student_loan_assistance_rules.temporary_provision: Current tax-free treatment under CARES Act and extensions; scheduled to expire after 2025

restrictions: Benefits exceeding $5,250 annual limit taxable as wages subject to income and payroll taxes
Cannot reimburse loan payments made by employee's relative; must be employee's own qualified education loans
Refinanced student loans qualify only to extent original loans were for qualified higher education expenses
Plan must be written, separate, and meet formal program requirements; informal payments don't qualify
Program cannot provide choice between taxable benefit and educational assistance (cash vs benefit test)
Graduate and undergraduate education loans qualify; no educational level or timing restrictions
Shareholder-employee ownership exceeding 5% triggers special nondiscrimination testing requirements
Post-2025 tax treatment uncertain without congressional extension; planning requires monitoring legislation
